constraints.description: Technology Readiness Level requirement: activities are expected to start at TRL 2 and achieve TRL 5 by the end of the project.
All proposals are expected to allocate tasks for cohesion activities with the European Partnership on AI, data, and robotics (ADRA).
All proposals are expected to allocate tasks for cohesion activities with the CSA HORIZON-CL4-2025-03-HUMAN-18: GenAI4EU central Hub.
All proposals are expected to incorporate mechanisms for assessing and demonstrating progress, including qualitative and quantitative KPIs, benchmarking, and progress monitoring.
Communicable results should be shared with the European R&D community through the AI-on-demand platform.
Projects selected in this topic should link to the resources offered by the AI Factories, including the Data Labs.

Participation is limited to legal entities established in EU Member States, Iceland, Norway, and the following associated countries: Canada, Israel, Republic of Korea, New Zealand, Switzerland, and the United Kingdom.
Entities controlled by non-eligible countries may be excluded from participation unless sufficient guarantees are provided.
The maximum EU contribution per selected project is EUR 19,000,000. The indicative total topic budget is EUR 38,000,000 supporting up to 2 projects.

Financial Support to Third Parties (FSTP) provisions apply: maximum EUR 300,000 per third party per grant at stage 2; maximum EUR 2,250,000 per third party per grant at stage 3; maximum EUR 2,550,000 per individual third party overall.

To ensure portfolio coverage, grants will be awarded not only in order of ranking score but also to at least one proposal focusing on each of the three sectors (healthcare, advanced manufacturing, in-vehicle autonomous driving), subject to all proposals passing evaluation thresholds.
When possible, proposals should build on and reuse public results from relevant previously funded actions.
Proposals should build on or seek collaboration with relevant projects and develop synergies with other relevant international, European, national, or regional initiatives.
This topic implements the co-programmed European Partnership on AI, data, and robotics (ADRA). Project results must contribute to the Apply AI Strategy objectives.
Maximum project duration is 48 months. This value is not explicitly stated in the topic text and is the standard maximum for large RIA actions under Horizon Europe.
constraints.assumption_note: The 48-month maximum is recorded in selected_call.json, which explicitly notes it is the standard maximum for large RIA actions under Horizon Europe and advises verification against General Annex B. The topic source documents (slice.json and curated extract) do not state a project duration limit. This constraint is assumed from the instrument type (RIA) and general programme rules, not confirmed from the topic-level source documents.
